prevalence.source: Indian Epilepsy Society / ICMR studies and IHME GBD estimates
prevalence.trend: stable; underdiagnosis and stigma contribute to persistent burden

incidence.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / Indian epidemiological studies

burden.mortalityRate: Elevated vs general population (SUDEP and injury-related)

vulnerablePopulations: rural populations with limited neurologist access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ILAE criteria; clinical history; EEG and imaging where available; differentiation from syncope/PNES

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis often delayed outside cities
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; large treatment gap (often >30%) due to access, cost, and stigma

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: EEG and neuroimaging concentrated in urban/tertiary centers; many rural diagnoses are clinical
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Affordable generics (phenytoin, carbamazepine, valproate, levetiracetam) widely used; referral for drug-resistant epilepsy evaluation where available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Neurologist scarcity, stigma, and out-of-pocket costs lead to poor adherence and delayed escalation for drug-resistant epilepsy
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of low-cost generic antiseizure medicines and growing tertiary epilepsy centers
